items.title: Heracles zit in overlevingsmodus
items.summary: Het kleine beetje vertrouwen dat Heracles Almelo in het bekertoernooi tankte, verdween zondag als sneeuw voor de zon. De ploeg van trainer Ernest Faber was kansloos in de derby tegen FC Twente en liet zich aftroeven in de duels. Zorgwekkend, vond ook Faber. Hij benadrukte na de wedstrijd dat er nu iets anders wordt gevraagd in Almelo.

items.title: Transfers Feyenoord: 'Timber mondeling akkoord met Olympique Marseille'
items.summary: VI volgt het transfernieuws bij Feyenoord op de voet, zeker nu de winterse transferwindow in aantocht is. De doelwitten van de Rotterdammers, duiding van de clubwatcher, of aandacht in buitenlandse media. We verzamelen alles hier.

items.title: 'Galatasaray blijft trekken aan Lang, vertrek bij Napoli waarschijnlijk'
items.summary: De tijd van winterse versterkingen is aangebroken, in dit blog volgen we alle transferactiviteit in het buitenland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Chaotic AFCON final an embarrassment to soccer
items.summary: Fan clashes, VAR controversies, a missed 'Panenka' penalty, a walkout, Senegal's extra-time winner over Morocco - the AFCON final had it all. But not in a good way.

items.title: Chaos at AFCON final after Senegal walk off
items.summary: The Africa Cup of Nations final went to extra time after finishing 0-0 in a chaotic end which saw Senegal players walk off the field when host Morocco was awarded a stoppage-time penalty.

items.title: De Jong slams arrogant ref's attitude in Barça loss
items.summary: Barcelona midfielder Frenkie de Jong attacked referee Jesús Gil Manzano's arrogant attitude after the LaLiga leader's 11-game winning run was ended by Real Sociedad on Sunday.

items.title: Cunha backs Carrick to succeed in new Utd 'cycle'
items.summary: Matheus Cunha is optimistic the team can go on a winning run under Michael Carrick after he led the side to a 2-0 win over City in his first game in charge.

items.title: Pulisic back in XI as Milan win, keep Inter in sight
London Spirit sign Overton, Dean, Zampa and reveal updated branding
items.summary: The Hundred's London Spirit have announced the signing of eight internationals for the 2026 campaign, while also revealing their 'new brand identity'.

items.title: Haller over crisis bij FC Utrecht: 'Het is een zeer pijnlijke worsteling'
items.summary: Op de bekeruitschakeling door FC Twente volgde een terechte 2-1 nederlaag tegen FC Volendam. Inmiddels is FC Utrecht afgegleden naar de tiende plaats in de Eredivisie. Het is crisis in de Domstad en de vertrekkende trainer Ron Jans krijgt het tij vooralsnog niet gekeerd. Wat is er aan de hand en wat moet er gebeuren? VI vroeg het aan Sébastien Haller.

items.title: Go Ahead Eagles grijpt toch naast Bergvall
items.summary: Go Ahead Eagles leek vorige week dicht bij de komst van Theo Bergvall, maar moet de jongeling toch aan zich voorbij laten gaan. De 21-jarige rechtsback van Djurgardens IF kiest voor FC Lausanne-Sport.

items.title: Scotland's home World Cup friendlies revealed
items.summary: Scotland will face Japan and Curacao in their two home friendlies ahead of the World Cup.

items.title: Ref Watch: AFCON chaos and should Dalot have seen red?
Frank expected to take charge of Spurs against Dortmund
items.summary: Thomas Frank is expected to remain in charge of Tottenham for their Champions League fixture against Borussia Dortmund on Tuesday night.

items.title: Shaka Hislop: ‘It might take another 100 years to dismantle racism but we’ll get there’
items.summary: Former Newcastle goalkeeper opens up on the abuse he has received and using the platform footballers have to support an anti-racism charity It was a chance encounter that would ultimately help change countless lives for the better but, at the time, all Shaka Hislop wanted to do was escape. As the then Newcastle goalkeeper stood on a petrol station forecourt, filling his car on a dark November night in 1995 his overriding emotions were outrage and fear. Hislop was heading home after an evening out with his wife and young daughter when, with the fuel gauge edging towards the red zone, he pulled into a garage just across the road from St James’ Park. Continue reading...

items.title: Premier League: 10 talking points from the weekend’s action
items.summary: Manchester United jog memories, Nick Woltemade comes up short and there’s a tough crowd for Chelsea’s owners Top scorers: check out the latest top-flight standings Sir Jim Ratcliffe was present to see the best victory and performance of his two years of minority ownership. When Ratcliffe bought in, the public impression given was of a billionaire signing up to taste the magic for himself. Saturday, and beating Manchester City, was an undoubted revival act where Michael Carrick’s team played the football of yore. That will almost certainly be unsustainable in the medium term, since most opposition will not play City’s high-line, high-wire act. But in engaging their supporters with determination and aggression, United jogged memories. There was a time when just about every big game had Old Trafford rocking like this, when the opposition could not hear themselves think. Surely that was the myth and legend Ratcliffe wanted to be part of? Would that be possible in the new stadium the Ineos chief has plans for instead of Old Trafford? Tottenham’s recent experiences suggest otherwise. Would Liverpool’s owners cash out the Anfield experience? Surely not. items.title: Mane slams 'sad' incident as Senegal's 'shameful' walk-off mars AFCON final
items.summary: Morocco head coach Walid Regragui says the image of Africa is "shameful" after Senegal walked off the pitch during the Africa Cup of Nations final when a penalty was awarded against them.

items.title: Chelsea open to selling Fernandez - Monday's gossip
items.summary: Chelsea tell Real Madrid they could sell Enzo Fernandez, Man Utd interested in Joao Gomes, and Nottingham Forest in talks for Lorenzo Lucca.

items.title: Carrick gave fans 'taste' of Ferguson era - Rooney
items.summary: Wayne Rooney says Man Utd's younger fans got a "taste of what it was like under Sir Alex Ferguson" in the win over Man City - and hopes it is a glimpse of what is to come under Michael Carrick.

items.title: Diaz will 'have nightmares' over 'Panenka' failure
items.summary: Brahim Diaz had the chance to win Afcon for Morocco, but his choice to try a Panenka ended up costing his side dearly as Senegal hit back to win the game in extra time.
